reviews.name: shreyas misale
reviews.text:
Worst stay ever – misleading photos, terrible hygiene & rude owner

Do NOT get fooled by the images, fancy reception, or the outside look of this hotel. The rooms are nothing like what is shown online. I am uploading real images for reference so others don’t fall into this trap.

Cleanliness: 0/5
Walls are full of stains. The room looks dirty, old, and completely neglected.

Hygiene: 0/5
Rooms smell bad all the time. Staying inside itself feels uncomfortable.

Bathroom condition is disgusting
Bathroom ceiling was leaking, and it looked like the bathroom hasn’t been cleaned properly for ages.

No hot water in freezing weather
Hot water is available only from 7 AM to 10 AM, which is ridiculous in a cold place like Ooty.

After returning from sightseeing at 6 PM, we requested the owner to turn on the geyser so we could freshen up. He rudely refused and repeated that hot water is only available in the morning.
We explained that the temperature was around 10°C and bathing with cold water was not possible. His response was shocking — “That’s not our concern.”

Room size is a joke
We booked a Deluxe Double Bed room, but the room is so small that only one person can stand at a time. If one person stands, the other must sit on the bed due to extreme congestion.

At this price, you will find much better, cleaner, and more comfortable hotels in Ooty with basic amenities and respectful staff.

Overall, this place is overpriced, unhygienic, misleading, and poorly managed.
Avoid this hotel at all costs.

hotelDescription:
Tucked away at the base of a hill and a 9-minute walk from the Anjuna Flea Market, this down-to-earth guesthouse also lies 5 km from Chapora Fort.
 The 12 simple, straightforward rooms with dark wood furniture offer en suite bathrooms, free Wi-Fi and flat-screen TVs, plus garden-view terraces and 24-hour room service.
 Amenities include a library under a banyan tree, a lounge area, shaded sunloungers and a lawn. On-site parking is free, but limited.

reviews.name: Aditya Jain
reviews.text:
Great Location, Clean Stay and Good Service – Scope for Better Amenities

My stay at Banyan Soul was overall value for money. The property is clean and comfortable. The owner and staff are highly responsive and genuinely try to provide the best possible service whenever approached.

The location is a strong advantage. There are several highly rated cafés nearby, and the market as well as the beach are within 5 minutes of driving, which makes it very convenient.

However, there are areas for improvement. The property does not offer breakfast or any meals. A paid breakfast option would significantly enhance the experience. At times, no one was present at the reception. Housekeeping is not proactive and needs to be requested rather than being done routinely.

Since there is no swimming pool and the bar counter area is not in use, that space could be converted into an activity area with indoor games to improve guest engagement.

Overall, a clean and well-located property with good service, but with scope to improve amenities and operational consistency.

hotelDescription:
Surrounded by woods and hills, this relaxed hotel is 7 km from State Highway 27, and 8 km from the museum and palace at the 17th-century Madikeri Fort, as well as Raja's Seat, a popular garden with musical fountain shows.
 Informal cottages have thatched roofs, and offer terraces with views of the hillside. Laid-back suites provide separate living rooms and 2 TVs.
 A warm restaurant with a terrace serves local, Chinese and Continental food. There’s also a bar, a game room, an indoor play area and a library. Additional amenities include an outdoor pool with sunloungers, plus a spa offering Ayurvedic treatments. Breakfast and Wi-Fi are available.

reviews.text: Excellent lodging in a great location in the special little town of Hampi. My room was large, clean, comfortable, and everything worked as it should; the property also has a large patio with outdoor seating for guests to enjoy. The owner is a great guy who will help ensure you have a rewarding stay. It's a short walk to the main temple, and ancient ruins up Hematuka Hill and down the Hampi Bazaar road. reviews.name: Sally Holbrook
reviews.text:
Not necessarily the physical property itself was my issue, it was other things that unfortunately ruined my days in Hampi. I would like to share that I have travelled a huge amount, to over 90 countries, and it is very rare for me to encounter what I did at Rocky’s. Normally I stay in dorm rooms at hostels, but decided to treat myself in Hampi, which was clearly a mistake. Never before have I been treated so badly.

I also need to mention that the place was extremely noisy. This was not due to guests or the road, but the staff themselves, who were shouting very loudly outside my room early in the morning. It was not occasional noise, it was loud and disruptive.

The main issue however was their Tuk Tuk driver, Eggy. While he may not technically be staff, he is effectively treated as such and clearly works closely with the property. He came and picked me up from the bus, which was no problem. The next day we agreed he would take me on a tour from 9am to 6pm, and we agreed a price of 1,600 rupees. I told him several times that I am very quick at going around places, but he insisted it would take nine hours. I agreed.

At the first temple I came out and could not see him. There were several Tuk Tuks there. He started shouting at me in Hindi to attract my attention, but I cannot understand the language, so I sent him a WhatsApp message. When I finally saw him, after maybe two minutes, he just kept talking at me. When I said I did not understand what he was saying and that I was sorry, he started to mimic me, no kidding, saying things like “I don’t understand Hindi” in a very sarcastic tone. This behaviour continued. Every time I said anything he repeated it back to me in a sarcastic and mimicking way, taking the Mickey out of my accent.

After about an hour of this I was nearly in tears, and I do not cry. I was so humiliated. I finished the whole tour in four hours, yet he still expected to charge me the full amount, even though the distance covered was not far in total. I then asked if we could do another area since I had technically agreed a price for the whole day. I would have been happy to pay the additional fuel cost, but he just shouted at me, called Rocky, and became even angrier.

In the end I paid 1,000 rupees, which was more than half of what we agreed. He was not happy. I got out of the Tuk Tuk and walked the rest of the way.

I would not stay here again and would strongly suggest staying elsewhere. For example, the place directly opposite has fabulous food and great temple views from the rooftop, and would have been a much better choice. It is called Gopi - and the people I spoke to who stayed there loved it! Book there 😊

Surrounded by jungle, this relaxed resort is 7 km from hiking trails at Aranamala Peak, 9 km from the scenic Soochipara Falls and 17 km from NH766 national highway.
 Simple, treehouse-style cottages come with flat-screen TVs, and balconies with jungle views; some feature skylights. Room service is available.

reviews.name: Santosh Lopez (SaN)
reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent stay – highly recommended!

Rai Cottage was really amazing. The stay was awesome. While booking, we had a few doubts, but once we reached the place, all of that disappeared. It’s a beautiful cottage right in the middle of nature—very peaceful and refreshing.

Special appreciation to the caretaker 👏 From the moment we arrived until checkout, he took care of all our needs without us having to ask twice. Truly great hospitality.

We were informed to place our dinner order by 11 AM, which felt a bit difficult initially (especially since we’re used to Bangalore Swiggy/Zomato 😄). But once we reached the location, we completely understood why. The food was absolutely worth the price and tasted amazing. A must-try: Chicken Sukka, Pork, and Rasam (yes, rasam 😄). Every dish was worth every penny.

The place is very affordable and well equipped—good Wi-Fi, TV, fire camp, and even homemade wine 🍷. We tried the Red Eye Chilli wine, and the best part? It actually helped cure our cough overnight!

Another big plus is the location. It’s perfectly situated between Madikeri and Kushalnagar, which made sightseeing very easy. We could split our days comfortably without any travel hassle.

Honestly, I don’t see any downside to this place. Everything is a big plus point.
Would definitely love to visit again!

The food was excellent—fresh, tasty, and home-style, which made our stay even better. A special thanks to Mani for the outstanding service and polite, helpful nature throughout our stay.

The owner was extremely welcoming and courteous, ensuring we were comfortable and well taken care of. Overall, it was a peaceful and pleasant stay, perfect for a family trip in Coorg.

Highly recommended, and we would love to visit again! 😊

My stay at Happy Estate was nothing short of magical. From the moment we arrived, we were enveloped in a sense of peace and natural beauty that is hard to find elsewhere.
The property itself is breathtaking. Waking up to the misty views of coffee plantations and the sounds of nature was the perfect escape from city life. The accommodation was comfortable, clean, and had a charming, rustic feel that complemented the surroundings perfectly.
What truly made our stay exceptional, however, was the incredible hospitality. Bagath is a fantastic host—warm, attentive, and always going the extra mile to ensure we had everything we needed. He shared great insights about the estate and Coorg, making our experience much more personal. The staff (John and the team )  were equally wonderful; they were incredibly helpful, friendly, and their recommendations for local sightseeing were spot-on.
Whether it was enjoying a freshly brewed cup of Coorg coffee on the porch or taking a leisurely walk through the estate, every moment was a delight. I cannot recommend Happy Estate highly enough.
If you are looking for a genuine nature retreat with exceptional hospitality, look no further. We left feeling rejuvenated and are already planning our next visit!

reviews.name: Monika Kashyap
reviews.text:
**Extremely Overpriced with Poor Hospitality & Cleanliness** - Not Worth It

I stayed here during the New Year's weekend and had a highly disappointing experience, especially considering the exorbitant price of ₹6,000 per night for a non-AC room.

The booking was made online with a "Pay at Property" policy. However, immediately after booking, we were aggressively hounded to pay for 3 of the 6 nights in advance, with threats of cancellation. Upon arrival, we were pressured again to pay the remaining balance upfront. This felt unusually transactional and soured our experience from the start.

The room was tiny and severely lacked basic amenities like cupboards. The towels provided were dirty and stained. Even after complaining, the replacements were also unclean. For a hostel charging premium rates, this level of neglect is unacceptable.

Basic hostel standards of cleanliness and frequent housekeeping were absent. On the third day, I had to request room cleaning twice. When they finally came, they only cleaned the bathroom, left the floor dirty, and did not change the bed linens.

The owner and manager seemed entirely distracted and focused only on invoicing. Hospitality and guest service were clearly their lowest priority, which was evident in their dismissive behaviour and conversations.

The only redeeming feature was a well-equipped kitchen, allowing guests to prepare their own breakfast before heading to the beach.

This hostel is a classic case of price gouging during peak season without delivering even basic value, service, or cleanliness. I strongly advise travelers to look elsewhere in Varkala for places genuine hospitality and reasonable pricing.

Rip-off!

Forced me to pay more than the given price on booking platform and refusing to give receipt.
Room was non AC and very basic.
Towels were old and dirty. You could hear everything that happened in the house and neighbouring bathroom.
Was told early check-in was okay at 11:30am but upon arrival was told it was not possible until 1pm.
Thank you for a disappointing stay...
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
First of all, we are a backpacking hostel, not a surf house. Our pricing during a long weekend was completely fair and in line with demand, you are grown up , nobody forced anything on you..We accommodated your stay despite you being kicked out of your previous property, which we did in good faith. Our rooms are NON-AC and were mentioned very clearly. Regarding the check-in , your room was ready long way back but you were on the beach and on check, you mentioned you will be checking in the evening. 
All towels provided were freshly cleaned. If there was any issue, it was never brought to our attention during your stay so it could be resolved.
We expect travelers to understand how places operate during busy weekends and to behave responsibly, rather than blaming the property for clearly stated conditions.

reviews.name: Manjunath Revanna
reviews.text:
A Truly Peaceful Getaway at Fernandes Homestay, Sakaleshpur

We had the pleasure of staying at Fernandes Homestay recently, and I must say it was one of the most peaceful and enjoyable experiences we’ve had in a long time. Nestled amidst the serene greenery of Sakaleshpur, the property offers a perfect blend of nature, comfort, and hospitality.

Right from the moment we arrived, Mr. Royston ensured that we were well taken care of. His warm and welcoming nature made us feel at home instantly. What really stood out was how genuinely attentive and accommodating he was throughout our stay. Every small request of ours was taken care of without hesitation, and always with a smile.

The food deserves a special mention — it was simply delicious! Each meal was freshly prepared with so much love and flavor, reflecting the local essence while also being homely and satisfying. We never once heard a "no" for anything we asked for, and that level of hospitality is rare to find these days.

The homestay itself is clean, well-maintained, and surrounded by lush nature that brings you a sense of calm and relaxation. Whether you're looking for a break from city life, a family outing, or a peaceful retreat with friends, this place has it all.

Highly recommended for anyone visiting Sakaleshpur. Thank you, Mr. Royston, for your exceptional hospitality — we will definitely be back!

reviews.name: Samiya Gupta
reviews.text:
A Dreamy Escape at Rivertree Farm Resort, Wayanad 🌿
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you are looking for more than just a place to sleep and want a true "life experience," Rivertree Farm Resort is exactly where you need to be. I recently stayed here and I am still buzzing from how incredible it was!

The Stay: A One-of-a-Kind Cave House
We stayed in their underground cave house, and honestly—OMG! It is such a "crazy" and brilliant concept. It’s a tribal-style design that feels authentic yet comfortable. The highlight for me was the private pool attached to the bedroom. Waking up and looking out at the 4-foot deep pool with a direct, open view of the coffee and pepper plantations was breathtaking. It felt like we were living right in the heart of nature, but with total privacy.

The Hospitality: Feeling Like Family
A massive shoutout to our host, Ajesh. He is hands-down one of the friendliest people I’ve met. He didn't just treat us like guests; he treated us like family. That kind of warmth is rare to find. Whether it was checking in on us or making sure everything was perfect, his hospitality made the trip 10x better.

The Food: Authentic & Delicious
The food was absolutely amazing. We had the complimentary breakfast, which was fresh and filling, and we also tried the homemade dinner (available for a nominal charge). You can tell it’s made with care—it tasted like a home-cooked feast every single time.

The Experience: So Much to Do!
What makes this place special is that it’s a "simple farm living" concept packed with adventure. We loved that so many activities were complimentary. During our stay, we got to enjoy:
• Kayaking & Bamboo Rafting: A peaceful way to connect with the water.
• Plantation Sunset Tour: The views of the greenery at dusk are unmatched.
• Rifle Shooting & Archery: Such a fun addition to a relaxing stay!
• Cycling: Great for exploring the surrounding farm life.

Final Verdict
This isn't just a resort; it’s a peaceful, relaxing stay in nature. No loud music or party vibes—just pure tranquility. If you want to escape the city and experience true Wayanad farm life, this is the spot.
Thank you, Ajesh and the team, for an unforgettable stay! We will definitely be back.

reviews.name: Archana Pai
reviews.text:
We celebrated our honeymoon at Rivertree Farmstay, and we couldn’t be happier with our choice of stay.

The quaint, peaceful atmosphere was the perfect backdrop for a romantic and relaxing break. The entire property is lush green and absolutely beautiful. We stayed at the cave house, which had been on my wishlist for over 2 years! It was absolutely worth it. The cave is beautiful on the outside and blissful on the inside. The little pool, while ice-cold, served as a convenient way of experiencing cold plunges in the midst of a forest-like vibe.

All the amenities inside the room were well put together. The bathroom was spotlessly clean, and well stocked with toiletries. The water heater worked well, as did the air conditioning. It was a thoughtful touch to have OTT platforms on the TV ready for our viewing experience without having to log in from our personal accounts.

Coming to management and service, we couldn’t have asked for a better host than Ajesh. He was kind, helpful, and so enthusiastic about providing us various experiences in and around his stay. He was a wonderful guide for the farm visit and toddy shop visit as well. His team was forthcoming and ever-ready to provide us assistance.

The food was delightful. We thoroughly enjoyed the fresh, home-cooked authentic Kerala food for breakfast (complimentary) and dinner. It was some of the best food I have ever had in Kerala.

We cannot recommend this place enough for a quaint, comfortable holiday in the heart of Kerala’s natural landscape.

address: Mallandur Rd, Bandekallu gudda, Jakkanahalli, Karnataka 577101, India

reviews.name: Grace N Glory
reviews.text:
My recent stay at The Heights was nothing short of extraordinary. Nestled amidst breathtaking scenery at an impressive altitude of 4800 meters, this homestay offers a truly unforgettable experience.
The accommodations were impeccable, providing a cozy and comfortable atmosphere that perfectly complemented the serene surroundings. The views from our room were simply breathtaking, with panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.
But what truly made our stay exceptional was the warm hospitality of our host, Santhosh. His knowledge of the local area and his genuine care for our well-being ensured that we had everything we needed. Santhosh's recommendations for nearby attractions and his assistance in arranging activities were invaluable.
Whether you're seeking adventure, relaxation, or simply a chance to escape the hustle and bustle of city life, The Heights is the perfect destination. I highly recommend this homestay to anyone looking for a truly memorable experience in Chikmagalur.

reviews.name: Rushank Muthreja
reviews.text:
A visit to The Height was the highlight of our trip to Chikmagalur! There's no better way I can think of to end our footballing trip than relaxing at the height of The Heights with friends and an absolutely amazing host!

The view from it's 5,000 ft elevation was breathtaking and just what one needed after a (very) steep kilometer long treck! I can't say enough about the view - it was heavenly!

The homestay itself has been very tastefully done - lovely seating arrangements - with a great view, comfortable tables and chairs made from natural wood, rugged, yet cozy tents...

What better dinner than freshly prepared steamed rice and chicken curry in the 10 degree temperature at that elevation! Straight from the wood fire to your plate! One could tell that the ingredients were fresh and the curry had an absolutely perfect mix of spices! Took me back home!

The host, Mr Santosh, made us feel at home and treated our group of guys (all 40+) as his friends - personally cooking for us and ensuring we're comfortable and having a great time!  Thankyou Santosh for this most amazing and memorable experience!

The Height is highly recommended  for nature enthusiasts and those that want relax and rise above the world!

I find myself constantly wishing to go back to experience our stay at Klusters again and again, that was the magic of Rabita and Soni's hosting.
Not only is this adorable cottage nestled amidst the vast forest, the hosts themselves are so wholesome and warm, they are the reason there is love and care found in every nook of this place.
Here we experienced the beauty of Virajpet. Each day was perfect, from cherishing the now-rare stillness of nature and it's serene orchestra, always sung by the choir of birds and insects; being greeted by their lovely dogs, to having delightful group conversations and equally delicious coffee, snacks and home-cooked dinners.
I remember that during a night at the bonfire, Rabs got us a plate of raw mangoes because I liked them so much. Such is their thoughtfulness and how sweet they are, whether it was very early in the morning or late at night, Soni and Rabita always took care of our needs.
Klusters Estate Stay is a perfect, enchanting getaway and was the most special for my beloved and I.
I would sure love to visit again a hundred times over. 🫶
🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎

reviews.name: Soumya Mukherjee
reviews.text:
Location Can’t Save a Sinking Ship
If real estate is the only thing that matters to you, Hotel Maurya Bhubaneswar has it in spades. Positioned perfectly for anyone needing to navigate the heart of the city, its location is—quite literally—its only saving grace. Unfortunately, once you step through the front doors, the experience goes downhill faster than a "dropped packet" of data.
While the rooms are decent and well-maintained, they are overshadowed by service standards that feel less like hospitality and more like an endurance test.
The "Service" (Or Lack Thereof)
The room service here can only be described as abysmal. Whether it’s a lack of training or a lack of staff, the result is a frustratingly slow and unresponsive experience. In a city known for its warmth, the service here feels cold and indifferent.
Bizarre Rules & Logistics
The hotel operates under a set of "house rules" that defy modern logic:
The Tea/Coffee Curfew: Good luck getting a caffeine fix in the evening. The hotel has a baffling rule prohibiting tea or coffee service after 5:30 PM.
Early Kitchen Closure: The kitchen shuts down at 9:30 PM, which is remarkably early for a city hotel. If you are arriving late from a long day of sightseeing or work, expect to go hungry or rely on delivery apps.
The Ice-less Bar: In perhaps the most confusing twist of all, the bar—an establishment designed to serve chilled drinks—does not have ice cubes. Trying to enjoy a drink here is a lesson in lukewarm disappointment.
A Groundhog Day Breakfast
While breakfast is included, don't expect variety. The menu repeats identically every single day. By day three, the lack of innovation becomes a chore rather than a meal.
The Verdict
The universe may not have an intent to harm us, but this hotel’s management certainly seems intent on testing your patience.
Pros: * Excellent, central location.
Decent, clean rooms.
Cons: * Frustratingly restricted service hours.
Repetitive dining options.
A general lack of basic amenities (like ice).
Final Word: Stay here only if you absolutely must be in this specific neighborhood and plan on spending every waking hour—including mealtimes—somewhere else.

⭐ Worst Hotel Experience Ever – Completely Misleading & Disappointing
I strongly advise everyone to think at least 10 times before booking Townhouse Madikeri.
We paid ₹7,000 for 2 nights (25th and 26th Jan 2026), expecting a decent stay based on the photos shown on Agoda. Unfortunately, the reality was completely opposite.
🔴 Fake & AI-enhanced photos
The pictures shown online are highly edited and misleading. The actual rooms look nothing like what is advertised.
🔴 Very old and broken furniture
The room furniture was extremely old, damaged, and poorly maintained. It felt like staying in a neglected lodge rather than a “Townhouse” hotel.
🔴 No basic amenities provided
No toiletries
No towels
TV not working
Room was smelly and unhygienic
For a paid hotel stay, these are the most basic expectations — and none were met.
🔴 OYO branding hidden during booking
Nowhere during booking was it clearly mentioned that this is an OYO property. This felt completely misleading.
🔴 False promises from customer support
When we complained to OYO customer care, they assured that the hotel manager had been contacted and someone would come to fix the issues “soon”.
No one came. Nothing was fixed.
These were just empty commitments to close the complaint.
Overall, the stay completely ruined our family trip experience in Coorg. This hotel does not justify even half of the amount charged.
❌ Not worth the money
❌ Poor maintenance
❌ False advertising
❌ Bad service
❌ No accountability
If you value your money, comfort, hygiene, and peace of mind — please avoid this property.
Absolutely not recommended

reviews.name: Eye Value
reviews.text:
⚠️ URGENT TRAVEL WARNING: DO NOT BOOK THIS HOTEL

I am writing this as a heartbroken and frustrated traveler to save anyone else from the nightmare I just experienced. If you are planning a trip to Goa with friends or family, avoid this property at all costs.
🚩 The "Scam" Cycle

This isn't just a bad hotel; it’s a calculated fraud. Since 2024, I have tracked this property changing its name four different times to wipe away hundreds of 1-star reviews and trick new victims. They have even falsified their Google Maps location to appear closer to the action, but in reality, it is nowhere near Vagator or the main party hubs.
🏨 Why This is a Disaster:

The "Catfish" Photos: The professional, beautified photos you see on booking sites are a complete lie. The reality is a decaying building that looks nothing like the advertisements.

Biohazard Washrooms: The bathrooms are beyond "dirty"—they are unsanitary and disgusting. Expect broken fixtures, foul smells, and grime that hasn't been touched in months.

The "Swamp" Pool: The swimming pool is a green, murky mess. It is a health hazard and completely unusable.

Dilapidated Rooms: The rooms are overpriced, poorly maintained, and feel like they haven't been cleaned in years.

Zero Connectivity: Because they faked their location, you will spend a fortune on taxis just to get to the actual beaches or clubs in North Goa.

Final Verdict: This place is a trap designed to steal your money and spoil your vacation memories. I lost my peace of mind and my hard-earned money here. Please, do your research, check the history of the "new" names popping up on maps, and do not book this hotel under any condition.

reviews.text:
I had a very poor experience here and would not recommend this hotel, especially for travelers coming from outside the area.

At the time of arrival, we were clearly told that early check-in was allowed. However, after some time, a staff member came and demanded extra payment for early check-in, which was never mentioned initially. This felt misleading and unprofessional.

The owner’s behavior was also unacceptable. From the beginning, he spoke very rudely and even started shouting when we said we would not pay the unexpected early check-in charge. There was no polite or respectful communication.

Another major issue was the inconsistency between staff and owner — staff members would say one thing, and later the owner would say something completely different. This created confusion and distrust.

Other problems we faced:
- Wi-Fi was not working
- Rooms are built too close to each other, making it difficult to get proper sunlight and natural ventilation.
- Owner were not helpful and very rude

Overall, it felt unsafe and dishonest. I strongly advise other outsiders and travelers to be careful before booking here.

The whole cottage doesn't feel like a commercial hotel, it's so homely and people who take care of this place. The owners are very sensible and took care of us like family members. The rooms and facilities are perfect, even the breakfast.. don't miss out on trying the biryani and parotha.

The best part is that it is at the right distance from main Ooty, not too far or close. It instantly feels secluded. The whole cottage has an incredible view of the valley.

reviews.name: Shalini Roy
reviews.text:
Hii. I am Shalini Roy. My Sister-in-law and I stayed in the property from 1st February to 5th February. It was overall a good stay. The staffs were helpful and they arranged whatever we needed. It was a safe stay. But there were some issues we want to mention specifically. First of all the property was not well maintained. It was not clean and not like the other property of Red Earth. Second when we gave them our room keys for cleaning when we were out, I found my bracelet was missing after returning. I informed them then and there and after 15-20 mins they came with the bracelet and informed me that by mistakenly they took it. I didn't expect that. And because it had elastic, I found that it got loose. N third when we asked for one particular person as our airport drop driver, first they told that they will inform but later they neither assign him nor inform us. Highly dissatisfied with this behaviour.
But overall whenever we asked for any help or room service they were there. The food was good and the other services were fine. Thank you!
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback, Shalini. We're genuinely glad your stay felt safe, that the team was helpful when you needed them, and that you enjoyed the food.

That said, we sincerely regret the concerns you've raised. On the matter of your bracelet - it was inadvertently picked up along with the bed linen and sent to the laundry. The moment this was noticed, it was returned to you immediately. We understand how unsettling that must have felt, and our team on the ground is deeply apologetic. We have since had a firm conversation with the housekeeping staff to ensure all linen is carefully checked before being moved to the laundry room - this should not happen again.

We also take note of your feedback regarding the upkeep and cleanliness of the property, as well as the miscommunication around your airport transfer request. These are not the standards we hold ourselves to, and we are addressing both with the team directly.

Your trust means a great deal to us, Shalini. Feedback like yours - specific and honest - is what helps us get better. We truly hope to have the chance to welcome you and your sister-in-law back and give you the experience that reflects the best of Red Earth Gokarna.

Team Red Earth
Kabini | Tadoba | Gokarna | Nilgiris | Wayanad

reviews.name: Randomly
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at Paradise Holiday Cottages in Gokarna, and it turned out to be one of the worst stays of my life.
The room had bed bugs, and I was badly bitten during the night. I am still suffering from painful, itchy bites even after returning home. This completely ruined my trip and caused serious discomfort.
When I informed the staff, instead of taking responsibility or even apologizing, they simply told me to “complain.” There was no refund, no room change, and no concern shown for my health or comfort. That level of negligence and lack of basic customer care is unacceptable.
Cleanliness is the most basic expectation from any accommodation, and clearly that standard is not being maintained here. Guests should not have to risk their health like this.
I would strongly advise others to think twice before booking here until proper hygiene standards and customer service improve.

On 8 hectares of rolling grounds surrounded by lush, forested mountains, this unpretentious, rural hotel lies 4 km from both Ooty Railway Station and the Government Botanical Gardens, and 6 km from the shores of Ooty Lake.
 Decorated with colourful fabrics, the warm 1- to 3-bedroom cottages, some with wood floors, offer living/dining areas, flat-screen TVs and minifridges, as well as garden terraces. Room service is available.
 There's a relaxed 24-hour restaurant offering scenic views and classic North Indian dishes.

reviews.text:
My stay at Fortune Retreats - Rose Villa (Single Bedroom) was truly excellent. The overall experience was comfortable and well-managed.
One of the most helpful amenities was the hot water facility. Having access to hot water in the living area was especially convenient, as I was traveling with my three-year-old daughter. It made sterilizing her essentials easy and stress-free, which I greatly appreciated.
The room itself offered a premium and well-maintained ambiance, adding to the comfort of our three-day, two-night stay. Everything felt thoughtfully arranged and guest-friendly.

reviews.name: Joseph C Joseph
reviews.text:
We were a group of 20 people (ten couples) who stayed at Fortune Retreats for three nights, and our overall experience was excellent.

The accommodation was comfortable, clean, and well-maintained. The rooms were sufficiently spacious with pleasant views, and housekeeping was prompt and efficient. The ambiance of the property was calm and relaxing, making it ideal for a group getaway.

The food quality was very good, with a wide variety of dishes catering to different tastes. The breakfast and dinner spreads were well-organized, fresh, and flavorful. The dining staff ensured that our large group was served smoothly without delay.

What stood out most was the courteous and professional attitude of the staff. From reception to restaurant service, everyone was polite, responsive, and eager to help. They handled our group arrangements efficiently, which made our stay comfortable and stress-free.

Overall, we had a memorable and enjoyable stay and would gladly recommend this property to others visiting Ooty.

description: Subdued rooms with views in a refined mountain resort, plus buffet dining & a gym.
address: Top Station Road, Ikka Nagar, Munnar, Kerala 685612, India
hotelDescription:
Set amidst lush greenery, this hilltop hotel, with a stately exterior and peaked roofs, is 3.1 km from the Carmelagiri Elephant Park, and 6 km from the Pothamedu Viewpoint.
 The elegant rooms and free-standing cottages, with hardwood floors and subdued tones, feature complimentary Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs, minibars, and tea and coffeemaking equipment. Cottages add balconies with valley views, and living and dining rooms.
 A complimentary breakfast buffet is served in a rustic-chic restaurant with panoramic views. Other amenities include an exercise room and conference facilities.
